title: Charmed Life - Chapter 17 (Fragment 5)
text:
“No,” said Cat. “Just see. Or he’d know all about you. I’m glad you’re staying behind. We can go and get the dragons’ blood while they’re at church.”

Janet kept watch at the window to see when the Family left. After about half an hour, she said, “Here they are at last, walking in a crowd down the avenue. All the men have got toppers, but Chrestomanci looks as if he’s come out of a shop window. Who are they all, Cat? Who’s the old lady in purple mittens, and the young one in green, and the little fellow who’s always talking?”

“I’ve no idea,” said Cat. He scrambled out of bed and scuttled up to his room to find some clothes. He felt perfectly well—marvelously well, in fact. He danced around his room while he put on his shirt. He sang putting on his trousers. Even the cold lump of dough on the carpet could not damp his spirits. He whistled tying his boots.

Janet came into the room as Cat was shooting out of it, pulling on his jacket and beaming with health. “I don’t know,” Janet said, as Cat shot past her and hammered away down the stairs. “Dying must agree with you, or something.”

“Hurry up!” Cat called from the bottom of the stairs. “It’s on the other side of the Castle from here. Millie says dragons’ blood is very dangerous, so don’t you touch it. I can spare a life on it and you can’t.”

Janet wanted to remark that Cat had not spared the last one very easily, but she never caught up with Cat sufficiently. Cat whirled through the green corridors and stormed up the winding stairs to Mr. Saunders’ room, and Janet only reached him when he was actually inside it. Then there was too much else to take up her attention.

The room was heavy with the scent of stale magic. Though it was much the same as when Cat had seen it before, Mr. Saunders had tidied it a little for Sunday. The cresset was out. The torts and limbecks and other vessels were all clean. The books and scrolls had been piled in heaps on the second bench. The five-pointed star was still there, blazoned on the floor, but there was a new set of signs chalked on the third bench, and the mummified animal had been neatly laid at one end of it.

Janet was immensely interested. “It’s like a laboratory,” she said, “except that it isn’t. What weird things! Oh, I see the dragons’ blood. Does he need all that huge jar? He won’t miss a bit out of that lot.”

There was a rustling at the end of the third bench. Janet’s head perked towards it. The mummified creature was twitching and spreading its filmy little wings.

“It did that before,” said Cat. “I think it’s all right.”

He was not so sure, however, when the creature stretched and got to its doglike feet, yawning. The yawn showed them dozens of small, sharp teeth and also let out a cloud of bluish smoke. The creature ran pattering along the bench towards them. The little wings rattled on its back as it came, and two small puffs of smoke streamed behind it from its nostrils. It stopped at the edge of the bench to look up at them inquisitively from a melting glitter of golden eyes. They backed nervously away from it.

vocabulary.definition: Radiating light or joy.
vocabulary.example: Pulling on his jacket and beaming with health.

vocabulary.definition: Displayed prominently or vividly.
vocabulary.example: The five-pointed star was still there, blazoned on the floor.

vocabulary.definition: Having a slight blue colour.
vocabulary.example: The yawn let out a cloud of bluish smoke.

vocabulary.definition: A band around the neck of a garment.
vocabulary.example: A shirt with a collar.

vocabulary.definition: Having a collar; caught or seized.
vocabulary.example: He was collared by the police.

vocabulary.definition: A metal cup or basket for holding burning fuel on a pole or wall bracket.
vocabulary.example: The cresset was out.

vocabulary.definition: To diminish the intensity of something.
vocabulary.example: Even the cold lump of dough on the carpet could not damp his spirits.

vocabulary.definition: Resembling a dog in characteristics or behaviour.
vocabulary.example: The creature got to its doglike feet.

vocabulary.definition: A bright, shimmering reflected light.
vocabulary.example: They stopped at the edge of the bench to look up at them inquisitively from a melting glitter of golden eyes.

vocabulary.definition: Large piles or masses of something.
vocabulary.example: The books and scrolls had been piled in heaps on the second bench.

vocabulary.definition: Curious or inquiring.
vocabulary.example: It stopped to look up at them inquisitively.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner showing curiosity or inquiring interest.
vocabulary.example: They backed nervously away from it after it looked up at them inquisitively.

vocabulary.definition: Special vessels or pot stills used in alchemy or chemistry.
vocabulary.example: The torts and limbecks and other vessels were all clean.

vocabulary.definition: Preserved from decay, especially by drying or embalming.
vocabulary.example: The mummified animal had been neatly laid at one end of the bench.

vocabulary.definition: Making light, rapid tapping sounds.
vocabulary.example: The creature ran pattering along the bench towards them.

vocabulary.definition: To say something as a comment.
vocabulary.example: Janet wanted to remark that Cat had not spared the last one very easily.

vocabulary.definition: Soft, muffled crackling sound caused by movement.
vocabulary.example: There was a rustling at the end of the third bench.

vocabulary.definition: Insufficient for the demand; rare or uncommon.
vocabulary.example: Scarce resources make survival difficult.

vocabulary.definition: Moved hurriedly or clumsily.
vocabulary.example: He scrambled out of bed and scuttled up to his room.

vocabulary.definition: Rolled up pieces of parchment or paper with writing.
vocabulary.example: The books and scrolls had been piled in heaps on the second bench.

vocabulary.definition: Moved quickly with short steps.
vocabulary.example: He scrambled out of bed and scuttled up to his room to find some clothes.

vocabulary.definition: Extending over a large area.
vocabulary.example: The mummified creature was twitching and spreading its filmy little wings.

vocabulary.definition: Moved angrily or forcefully.
vocabulary.example: Cat whirled through the corridors and stormed up the winding stairs.

vocabulary.definition: A type of formal hat.
vocabulary.example: All the men have got toppers.

vocabulary.definition: Small flat heating pans or vessels used in alchemical or laboratory work.
vocabulary.example: The torts and limbecks and other vessels were all clean.

vocabulary.definition: Making small, sudden jerking movements.
vocabulary.example: The mummified creature was twitching and spreading its filmy little wings.

vocabulary.definition: Moved rapidly around and around.
vocabulary.example: Cat whirled through the green corridors.

vocabulary.definition: To open one's mouth wide and breathe in deeply, typically as a sign of tiredness or boredom.
vocabulary.example: The creature stretched and got to its doglike feet, yawning.
